prompt:
Master-data team ticket. Our partner-onboarding service needs to read business partners out of S/4HANA and every past attempt was someone string-concatenating OData URLs until the gateway stopped complaining — no paging, no $expand, and when SAP returns one of its layered error documents we log 'HTTP 400' and throw the details away, which support loves. Build the client properly as a set of Java classes next to TestMain.java (default package, stdlib only — java.net.http plus your own small JSON handling; we run everything with the source launcher). TestMain.java is the acceptance contract; it and everything under docs/ are protected.

Research comes first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to confirm the CURRENT official docs for the S/4HANA Business Partner (A2X) API — the SAP Business Accelerator Hub entry for API_BUSINESS_PARTNER (current version, service path, what the service supports), and since it is an OData V2 service, the OData 2.0 URI-conventions and JSON-format specification pages SAP's docs defer to: how $select/$filter/$expand compose (including what happens to a single quote INSIDE a string literal), how a collection response is wrapped, what __count, __next, and __deferred mean, and how Edm.DateTime values look on the wire. SAP's Cloud SDK documentation (sap.github.io/cloud-sdk) is SAP's official statement of how their gateways page and report errors — read it. First-party sources only (api.sap.com, help.sap.com, SAP's official GitHub/SDK docs, odata.org for the protocol). docs/contract.json pins our mock's wire contract, docs/official_sources.json the provenance; if live docs contradict a pin, flag it in your summary and leave protected files alone. Cite the exact official URLs you actually read in your final message.

Shape the tests compile against (internal design beyond this is yours; expect ~5 classes):
- ODataQuery.entitySet(String) with .select(String...), .filterEq(String, String) (calls accumulate; think about what the protocol does with quotes inside values), .expand(String...), .top(int), .skip(int), .inlinecountAllPages(), .toPath() producing the exact encoded path+query the tests pin — option order and space encoding matter.
- SapODataClient(serviceRootUrl, user, password) with FetchResult fetchAll(ODataQuery) — requests JSON, authenticates every hop, follows the server's partial-set continuation exactly as returned (our mock plants a marker param that a rebuilt URL loses), accumulates entities in order — and List<ODataEntity> fetchDeferredCollection(String uri) for navigation properties that arrived unexpanded.
- ODataEntity with str(name), instant(name) (mind the wire format for dates), hasDeferred(nav), deferredUri(nav), inline(nav) (expanded collections arrive wrapped — unwrap them).
- FetchResult with entities() and inlineCount() (Long, null when not requested).
- SapODataException for non-2xx responses: httpStatus(), code(), the human message in getMessage(), and errorDetail(String... keys) navigating the COMPLETE preserved error document — SAP nests a lot under innererror and support needs all of it. Credentials must never appear in exception text.
- BusinessPartnerReport.wholesaleSnapshot(SapODataClient) — one paged query for organization partners (category '2') selecting id+name with addresses expanded, resolving any address list the server left deferred, returning 'partnerId|name|City/Country;City/Country' lines sorted by partner id.

Acceptance: java TestMain.java — must print the final OK line. Loopback mock only, dummy communication user, no sleeps, no wall-clock dependence.

research.product: SAP S/4HANA Cloud — Business Partner (A2X) OData API

official_sources.title: SAP Business Accelerator Hub catalog record — Business Partner (A2X), API_BUSINESS_PARTNER 1.7.0
official_sources.used_for: current API version (1.7.0), ODATA service code, service URL .../sap/opu/odata/sap/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER, A2X CRUD scope over Business Partner/Supplier/Customer, deep-entity and batch support statement, pointer to the Business Partner (A2X) help topic

official_sources.title: URI Conventions (OData Version 2.0)
official_sources.used_for: $select/$filter/$expand/$top/$skip/$inlinecount syntax; eq/and operators and single-quoted string literals; comma-separated $expand paths with slash nesting; $inlinecount=allpages semantics; %20 percent-encoding of spaces

official_sources.title: JSON Format (OData Version 2.0)
official_sources.used_for: d wrapper and results array; __count; __next partial-set link; __deferred representation of unexpanded navigation properties; inline expanded entries; /Date(ms)/ Edm.DateTime encoding

official_sources.title: Use the OData v2 Type-safe Client API | SAP Cloud SDK for Java (official SAP SDK docs)
official_sources.used_for: SAP OData V2 server-driven paging semantics ($skiptoken next links indicate further pages; the SDK resolves all pages); SAP service errors surfaced with the OData error details preserved (ODataServiceErrorException/getODataError) — grounding for preserving code/message/innererror verbatim

official_sources.title: Operations (OData Version 2.0)
official_sources.used_for: error conditions exposed as 4xx/5xx responses whose bodies carry detailed error information; response-code conventions for the protocol SAP Gateway implements

research.language_rationale: The master-data tooling this client joins is a Java service; SAP's own Java integration surface (SAP Cloud SDK) wraps exactly this OData V2 wire protocol, so implementing the documented protocol directly with java.net.http and a small parser is credible and keeps the hermetic seed dependency-free, while the Cloud SDK docs serve as SAP's normative description of pagination and error semantics.
